title: Davenport Creek Cold Plunge
body:
Kristin lived in her body.

When she was hit with cancer, it was extra painful for her because her body betrayed her. But while she was fighting cancer, and for her whole life before, she lived sensually. She obviously enjoyed dancing and moving but she also enjoyed listening, singing, heat, and cold.

One day I came out to visit her in Davenport and she persuaded me to do a cold plunge with her. Stepping into ice cold water is painful and shocking. It's even harder when you're doing it in a little creek in Davenport where there is no easy way to hop right in but you have to navigate rocks and vines.

But once we got in up to our necks... the pain passed and we moved into breathing and flowing. The blood leaves the skin and circulates. The racing thoughts give way to calm rhythmic waves. The pain evaporates.

And then we got out and our cells buzzed.

We see life through the windows of our eyes and the fuzzy sounds in our ears. It feels infinite and safe. Until a tear forms in the veil of our comfort. A cold plunge is a tear in the comfort. It brings forth the sense of how wonderfully precious life is. How lucky we are to love and be loved.

We were two kids in adult bodies sharing the experience of tearing the veil of our comfort and gasping and giggling as we face the greater reality.

If it's sunny and warm out, go to the Davenport creek. Bring a towel and a friend. Get in. Even if it is shocking and painful at first. Get in. Tear the veil. Kristin is right there on the other side.
